name: apocalypse-never
description: Michael Shellenberger's 'Apocalypse Never: Why Environmental Alarmism Hurts Us All' — a controversial critique of environmental alarmism, arguing that fear-based messaging distorts science, leads to bad policy, and makes real environmental progress harder to achieve.
useCase: Understand the case against environmental alarmism, the role of nuclear energy in decarbonization, and how pragmatic environmentalism can achieve better results than fear-based approaches.
knownRisks: Highly controversial — many environmentalists strongly disagree with Shellenberger's arguments
Shellenberger has been accused of downplaying climate risks by some critics
Nuclear energy advocacy is disputed — concerns about waste and cost remain
